caching.description: "true" if changed to the keyValue store need to be buffered and periodically released, "false" to emit all changes directly

historyRetention.description: (Versioned only) The duration for which old record versions are available for query (cannot be negative)

keyType.description: The key type of the keyValue store

logging.description: "true" if a changelog topic should be set up on Kafka for this keyValue store, "false" otherwise

name.description: The name of the keyValue store. If this field is not defined, then the name is derived from the context.

persistent.description: "true" if this keyValue store needs to be stored on disk, "false" otherwise

segmentInterval.description: Size of segments for storing old record versions (must be positive). Old record versions for the same key in a single segment are stored (updated and accessed) together. The only impact of this parameter is performance. If segments are large and a workload results in many record versions for the same key being collected in a single segment, performance may degrade as a result. On the other hand, historical reads (which access older segments) and out-of-order writes may slow down if there are too many segments.

timestamped.description: "true" if elements in the store are timestamped, "false" otherwise

type.description: The type of the state store, fixed value "keyValue"

valueType.description: The value type of the keyValue store

versioned.description: "true" if elements in the store are versioned, "false" otherwise
